name: BI_AltmanZScore
comment: Calculates Altman Z Score. Use BI_AltmanZScoreVariables() function to list the variables.
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.Data,_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.NWC,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,1),_xlpm.TA,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,2),_xlpm.RE,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,3),_xlpm.EBIT,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,4),_xlpm.MC,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,5),_xlpm.TL,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,6),_xlpm.S,INDEX(_xlpm.Data,7),_xlpm.a,_xlpm.NWC/_xlpm.TA, _xlpm.b, _xlpm.RE/_xlpm.TA, _xlpm.c, _xlpm.EBIT/_xlpm.TA, _xlpm.d, _xlpm.MC/_xlpm.TL,_xlpm.e,_xlpm.S/_xlpm.TA,_xlpm.zscore,1.2* _xlpm.a + 1.4*_xlpm.b+3.3*_xlpm.c+0.6*_xlpm.d+_xlpm.e,_xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("Altman Z Score",_xlpm.zscore),_xlfn.HSTACK("Verdict",_xlfn.IFS(_xlpm.zscore>=3,">= 3 - Safe Zone - Low likelyhood of Bankruptcy",_xlpm.zscore<1.81,"<1.81 - Distress Zone – High Likelihood of Bankruptcy",_xlpm.zscore>=1.81,"Middle - Grey Zone – Moderate Risk of Bankruptcy")))))

name: BI_AltmanZScoreVariables
comment: Lists out Altman Z Score Variables.
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA({"Working Capital";"Total Assets";"Retained Earnings";"Earnings Before Interest and Taxes";"Market Value";"Total Liabilities";"Sales"})

name: BI_AnovaSingleFactor
comment: Calculates Anova (Analysis of Variance) Single Factor. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_AnovaTwoFactorsWithoutReplication
comment: Calculates Anova (Analysis of Variance) without replication. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_AnsCombeQuartet
comment: Shows AnsCombe Quartet. Anscombe's quartet is a group of four data sets that are nearly identical in simple descriptive statistics, but there are peculiarities that fool the regression model once you plot each data set.

name: BI_ArrayContains
comment: Verifies whether one record of data contains in a table and returns true or false.

name: BI_ArrayCountif
comment: Counts howmany times a record of data repeated in a table of data. If not found returns 0.

name: BI_ArrayFilter
comment: Filters a Table for a multi-column criteria array by matching selected columns with the criteria array.

name: BI_ArrayInArray
comment: Filters only the available rows in an array found in another array.

name: BI_ArrayMatch
comment: Returns the row number of an array where a matching row is first found. Returns #NA if not found.

name: BI_ASort
comment: Returns the sorted array by all the columns  in the order entered. Useful for sorting Countrywise, Regionwise, Statewise, Citywise etc.,

name: BI_AttributeUnitCount
comment: Returns Attribute Unit Sampling cumulative count of any attribute.

name: BI_BeginsWith
comment: Returns whether one text begins with another text.

name: BI_BeneishMScore
comment: Calculates Beneish M Score. Beneish's M-Score is a mathematical model that uses eight financial ratios weighted by coefficients to identify whether a company has manipulated its profits.
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.LastYear,_xlpm.CurrentYear,_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.LYNS,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,1),_xlpm.CYNS,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,1),_xlpm.LYCGS,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,2),_xlpm.CYCGS,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,2),_xlpm.LYSGA,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,3),_xlpm.CYSGA,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,3),_xlpm.LYNI,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,4),_xlpm.CYNI,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,4),_xlpm.LYPPE,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,5),_xlpm.CYPPE,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,5),_xlpm.LYDEP,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,6),_xlpm.CYDEP,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,6),_xlpm.LYCA,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,7),_xlpm.CYCA,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,7),_xlpm.LYNR,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,8),_xlpm.CYNR,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,8),_xlpm.LYTA,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,9),_xlpm.CYTA,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,9),_xlpm.LYLD,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,10),_xlpm.CYLD,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,10),_xlpm.LYCL,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,11),_xlpm.CYCL,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,11),_xlpm.LYCF,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYear,12),_xlpm.CYCF,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYear,12),_xlpm.LYOLTA,(_xlpm.LYTA-(_xlpm.LYCA+_xlpm.LYPPE)),_xlpm.CYOLTA,(_xlpm.CYTA-(_xlpm.CYCA+_xlpm.CYPPE)),_xlpm.DSRI,(_xlpm.CYNR/_xlpm.CYNS)/(_xlpm.LYNR/_xlpm.LYNS),_xlpm.GMI,((_xlpm.LYNS-_xlpm.LYCGS)/_xlpm.LYNS)/((_xlpm.CYNS-_xlpm.CYCGS)/_xlpm.CYNS),_xlpm.AQI,(_xlpm.CYOLTA/_xlpm.CYTA)/(_xlpm.LYOLTA/_xlpm.LYTA),_xlpm.SGI,_xlpm.CYNS/_xlpm.LYNS,_xlpm.DEPI,(_xlpm.LYDEP/(_xlpm.LYDEP+_xlpm.LYPPE))/(_xlpm.CYDEP/(_xlpm.CYDEP+_xlpm.CYPPE)),_xlpm.SGAI,(_xlpm.CYSGA/_xlpm.CYNS)/(_xlpm.LYSGA/_xlpm.LYNS),_xlpm.LVGI,((_xlpm.CYLD+_xlpm.CYCL)/_xlpm.CYTA)/((_xlpm.LYLD+_xlpm.LYCL)/_xlpm.LYTA),_xlpm.TATA,(_xlpm.CYNI-_xlpm.CYCF)/_xlpm.CYTA,_xlpm.MScore8,-4.84+0.92*_xlpm.DSRI+0.528*_xlpm.GMI+0.404*_xlpm.AQI+0.892*_xlpm.SGI+0.115*_xlpm.DEPI-0.172*_xlpm.SGAI-0.327*_xlpm.LVGI+4.679*_xlpm.TATA, _xlpm.MScore5, -6.065 + 0.823*_xlpm.DSRI + 0.906*_xlpm.GMI + 0.593*_xlpm.AQI + 0.717*_xlpm.SGI + 0.107*_xlpm.DEPI,_xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("DSRI",_xlpm.DSRI),_xlfn.HSTACK("GMI",_xlpm.GMI),_xlfn.HSTACK("AQI",_xlpm.AQI),_xlfn.HSTACK("SGI",_xlpm.SGI),_xlfn.HSTACK("DEPI",_xlpm.DEPI),_xlfn.HSTACK("SGAI",_xlpm.SGAI),_xlfn.HSTACK("LVGI",_xlpm.LVGI),_xlfn.HSTACK("TATA",_xlpm.TATA),_xlfn.HSTACK("Beneish M Score 8 Variables",_xlpm.MScore8),_xlfn.HSTACK("Verdict",IF(_xlpm.MScore8<-2.22,"Since M Score (8 Variables) is less than -2.22, the company is not likely to have manipulated its earnings", "Since the M Score is more than -2.22, the company is likely to have manipulated its earnings")),_xlfn.HSTACK("Beneish M Score 5 Variables",_xlpm.MScore5), _xlfn.HSTACK("Verdict",IF(_xlpm.MScore5<-2.22,"Since M Score (5 Variables) is less than -2.22, the company is not likely to have manipulated its earnings", "Since the M Score is more than -2.22, the company is likely to have manipulated its earnings")))))

name: BI_BeneishMScoreVariables
comment: Lists out Beneish M Score variables to make it easy to enter two years data for calculating Beneish M Score.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_FirstDigit
comment: Returns Benford Law for the first digit of a data set.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_FirstThreeDigits
comment: Returns Benford Law for the first three digits of a data set.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_FirstTwoDigits
comment: Returns Benford Law for the first two digits of a data set.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_LastTwoDigits
comment: Returns Benford Law for the last two digits of a data set.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_SecondDigit
comment: Returns Benford Law for the second digit of a data set.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_SecondOrder
comment: Calculates Benford Law Second Order - Frequency, Actual and Expected values along with Mean Abstract Deviation.

comment: Calculates Benford Law Summary Test - Frequency, Actual and Expected values along with Mean Abstract Deviation.

name: BI_BenfordLaw_ThirdDigit
comment: Returns Benford Law for the third digit of a data set.

name: BI_BottomN
comment: Returns Bottom N ranked rows of a column of data.

name: BI_BottomPercent
comment: Returns Bottom N Percentage of Data

name: BI_Consolidate
comment: Multiple Consolidation Ranges

name: BI_Contains
comment: Returns true if FindText is found in WithinText.

name: BI_Correlation
comment: Returns a Correlation Table of many attributes. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_Covariance
comment: Returns a Covariance Table of many attributes. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_DateDif
comment: Returns Age in years, months, days etc.,

name: BI_Describe
comment: Returns the descriptive statistics of Data.

Error",_xlfn.STDEV.S(_xlpm.Range)/(COUNT(_xlpm.Range)^0.5)), _xlfn.HSTACK("Population Variance",_xlfn.VAR.P(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Sample Variance",_xlfn.VAR.S(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Kurtosis",KURT(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Skewness.P",_xlfn.SKEW.P(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Skewness S",SKEW(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Range",MAX(_xlpm.Range)-MIN(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Maximum",MAX(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Minimum",MIN(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Sum",SUM(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.HSTACK("Count",COUNT(_xlpm.Range)), _xlfn.LET(_xlpm.Quart1,_xlfn.QUARTILE.INC(_xlpm.Range,1), _xlpm.Quart3, _xlfn.QUARTILE.INC(_xlpm.Range,3), _xlpm.IQR, _xlpm.Quart3-_xlpm.Quart1, _xlpm.wiqr15, _xlpm.IQR * 1.5, _xlpm.iqrlb, MAX(_xlpm.Quart1-_xlpm.wiqr15,MIN(_xlpm.Range)),_xlpm.iqrub, MIN(_xlpm.Quart3+_xlpm.wiqr15,MAX(_xlpm.Range)),_xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("Quartile1",_xlpm.Quart1), _xlfn.HSTACK("Quartile3",_xlpm.Quart3),_xlfn.HSTACK("IQR",_xlpm.IQR), _xlfn.HSTACK("1.5 Times IQR",_xlpm.wiqr15), _xlfn.HSTACK("Whisker Lower Bound",_xlpm.iqrlb), _xlfn.HSTACK("Whisker Outer Bound",_xlpm.iqrub)))))

name: BI_DiscountedPayback
comment: Returns the Discounted Payback period for a series of cashflows

name: BI_EMISchedule
comment: Returns EMI Schedule of a loan

name: BI_EndsWith
comment: Returns whether one text ends with another text.

name: BI_ExactArray
comment: Compares two arrays and returns whether both the arrays are equal.

name: BI_ExponentialSmoothing
comment: Returns Exponential Smoothened data after applying Damping Factor. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_ExtractNumbers
comment: Extracts all the numbers from a text string.

name: BI_ExtractText
comment: Extracts all the characters from a  text string excluding numbers.

name: BI_FDRSchedule
comment: Returns the Fixed Deposit Schedule of Interest calculations and maturiy value.

name: BI_FillDown
comment: Fills down blank cells with the content above.

name: BI_FinancialYear
comment: Returns  the financial year (1st April to 31st March) of any date.

name: BI_FinancialYearEnd
comment: Returns  the financial year end (31st March) of any date.

name: BI_FinancialYearStart
comment: Returns  the financial year start (1st April) of any date.

name: BI_FindDuplicates
comment: Returns duplicate values from a list

name: BI_FindMissingNumbers
comment: Returns missing numbers from a list of numbers

name: BI_FindOneOf
comment: Returns the position of the first specified character value in a string. If the specified value is not found, 0 is returned. CaseWare IDEA inspiration.

name: BI_FindUnique
comment: Returns the values available only once (without duplicates) in a list.

name: BI_FormulaList
comment: Returns the formula list in the specified range.

name: BI_Fuzzy
comment: Returns the number of modifications required to change from first text to second text. This finds Levenshtein distance between two strings.

name: BI_GEL1Test
comment: Calculates Gestalt Element Link Test between two columns of related data like Salesman and Customer

name: BI_GEL2Test
comment: Calculates Gestalt Element Link Test 2 between two columns of related data like Salesman and Customer for a particular dataitem.

name: BI_GenerateRandom
comment: Function to generate static random numbers between 0 and 1 using a Random Seed. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_GenerateRandomBetween
comment: Function to generate static random numbers between two numbers using a Random Seed. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_Histogram
comment: Function to create Histogram analysis. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

name: BI_OhlsonsOScore
comment: Calculate Ohlsons O Score
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.Data,_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.ta, INDEX(_xlpm.Data,1), _xlpm.GNP, INDEX(_xlpm.Data,2), _xlpm.TL, INDEX(_xlpm.Data,3), _xlpm.WC, INDEX(_xlpm.Data,4), _xlpm.CL, INDEX(_xlpm.Data, 5), _xlpm.CA, INDEX(_xlpm.Data, 6), _xlpm.LYNI, INDEX(_xlpm.Data, 7), _xlpm.CYNI, INDEX(_xlpm.Data,8), _xlpm.FFO, INDEX(_xlpm.Data, 9), _xlpm.Oscore, -1.32 -0.407*LOG(_xlpm.ta/_xlpm.GNP)+6.03*(_xlpm.WC/_xlpm.ta)+0.0757*(_xlpm.CL/_xlpm.CA)-1.72*(IF(_xlpm.TL>_xlpm.ta,1,0))-2.37*(_xlpm.CYNI/_xlpm.ta)-1.83*(_xlpm.FFO/_xlpm.TL)+0.285*(IF(AND(_xlpm.LYNI<0, _xlpm.CYNI<0),1,0))-0.521*((_xlpm.CYNI-_xlpm.LYNI)/(ABS(_xlpm.LYNI)+ABS(_xlpm.CYNI))),_xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("Ohlsons O Score",_xlpm.Oscore), _xlfn.HSTACK("Verdict",IF(_xlpm.Oscore > 0.5, "The firm will default within two years.", "The firm is not likely to default within two years.")))))

name: BI_OhlsonsOScoreVariables
comment: Calculates Ohlsons O Score. It is used for predicting bankruptcy is a multi-factor financial formula postulated in 1980 by Dr. James Ohlson of the New York University
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA({"Total Assets";"Gross National Product Price Level Index";"Total Liabilities";"Working Capital";"Current Liabilities";"Current Assets";"LastYear Net Income";"Current Year Net Income";"Funds From Operations"})

name: BI_PaybackPeriod
comment: Returns the payback period from series of cash flows

name: BI_PiotroskiFScore
comment: Calculates Piotroski F-score. Use BI_PiotroskiFScoreVariables() to list the variables and enter two years data
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.LastYearData,_xlpm.CurrentYearData,_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.LYNI,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData,1),_xlpm.CYNI,INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,1), _xlpm.LYOPTA,INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData,2), _xlpm.CYOPTA, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,2), _xlpm.LYCLTA, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData,3), _xlpm.CYCLTA, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,3), _xlpm.LYCF, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData,4), _xlpm.CYCF, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,4), _xlpm.LYLTD, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData,5), _xlpm.CYLTD, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,5), _xlpm.LYCA, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData, 6), _xlpm.CYCA, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData,6), _xlpm.LYCL, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData, 7), _xlpm.CYCL, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData, 7), _xlpm.LYEQ, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData, 8), _xlpm.CYEQ, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData, 8), _xlpm.LYNS, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData, 9), _xlpm.CYNS, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData, 9), _xlpm.LYGPR, INDEX(_xlpm.LastYearData, 10), _xlpm.CYGPR, INDEX(_xlpm.CurrentYearData, 10), _xlpm.RoA, IF(_xlpm.CYNI > 0, 1, 0), _xlpm.CFO, IF(_xlpm.CYCF>_xlpm.LYCF, 1, 0), _xlpm.ROAChange, IF((_xlpm.CYNI/_xlpm.CYCLTA)>(_xlpm.LYNI/_xlpm.LYCLTA),1,0),_xlpm.CashFlowROA, IF(_xlpm.CYCF>_xlpm.CYNI,1,0),_xlpm.leverage,IF((_xlpm.CYLTD/((_xlpm.CYOPTA + _xlpm.CYCLTA)/2))<(_xlpm.LYLTD/((_xlpm.LYOPTA+_xlpm.LYCLTA)/2)),1,0),_xlpm.CR, IF(_xlpm.CYCA/_xlpm.CYCL > _xlpm.LYCA/_xlpm.LYCL, 1, 0), _xlpm.eq, IF(_xlpm.CYEQ>_xlpm.LYEQ, 0, 1), _xlpm.GPR, IF(_xlpm.CYGPR > _xlpm.LYGPR, 1, 0 ), _xlpm.AssetTO, IF((_xlpm.CYNS/_xlpm.CYOPTA)>(_xlpm.LYNS/_xlpm.LYOPTA),1, 0), _xlpm.FScore, _xlpm.RoA + _xlpm.CFO + _xlpm.ROAChange + _xlpm.CashFlowROA + _xlpm.leverage + _xlpm.CR + _xlpm.eq + _xlpm.GPR + _xlpm.AssetTO,_xlpm.result,_xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("Positive Net Income", _xlpm.RoA), _xlfn.HSTACK("Positive Cash Flow", _xlpm.CFO), _xlfn.HSTACK( "Change in Return on Assets", _xlpm.ROAChange), _xlfn.HSTACK("Cash Flow over Net Income", _xlpm.CashFlowROA), _xlfn.HSTACK("Leverage - Long term debt over Average Assets", _xlpm.leverage ), _xlfn.HSTACK("Liquidity - Current Ratio", _xlpm.CR), _xlfn.HSTACK("Total Equity", _xlpm.eq), _xlfn.HSTACK("GP Ratio", _xlpm.GPR), _xlfn.HSTACK( "Asset Turnover", _xlpm.AssetTO), _xlfn.HSTACK("Total Score", _xlpm.FScore), _xlfn.HSTACK("Verdict", _xlpm.FScore &" Out of 9 - " & IF(_xlpm.FScore>=8,"Strong!",IF(_xlpm.FScore<=2,"Weak", "Average"))) ),_xlpm.result))

name: BI_PiotroskiFScoreVariables
comment: Lists out Piotroski F Score variables to make it easy to enter two years data for calculating Piotroski F Score.
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA({"Net Income";"Opening Total Assets";"Closing Total Assets";"Cashflow from Operations";"Long Term Debt";"Current Assets";"Current Liabilities";"Common Equity";"Net Sales";"GP Ratio"})

name: BI_Pivot
comment: Creates a Pivot Table from Data.

name: BI_Quarter
comment: Returns the Quarter number for a date.

name: BI_QuarterEnd
comment: Returns the Quarter ending date.

name: BI_QuarterStart
comment: Returns the Quarter Starting date.

name: BI_Rank.Dense
comment: Returns the Rank without any missing rank when there is a tie.

name: BI_Regression
comment: Calculates the Linear Regression. This is one of the Data Analysis Toolpak features.

_xlpm.conf, _xlpm.resdf),_xlpm.u95u,_xlpm.coeff+_xlpm.serr* _xlfn.T.INV.2T(1-_xlpm.conf, _xlpm.resdf),_xlpm.el, _xlfn.BYCOL(_xlfn.SEQUENCE(1,9),_xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,"")),_xlpm.e7l, _xlfn.BYCOL(_xlfn.SEQUENCE(1,7),_xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,"")),_xlpm.obs,_xlfn.SEQUENCE(_xlpm.count),_xlpm.pr,_xlfn.BYROW(_xlfn.HSTACK(_xlfn.SEQUENCE(_xlpm.count,1,1,0),_xlpm.DX),_xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,SUMPRODUCT(_xlpm.x,TRANSPOSE(_xlpm.coeff)))),_xlpm.res,_xlpm.DY-_xlpm.pr, _xlpm.result, _xlfn.VSTACK(_xlfn.HSTACK("Summary Output","",_xlpm.e7l), _xlpm.el,_xlfn.HSTACK("Regression Statistics","",_xlpm.e7l),_xlfn.HSTACK("Multiple R",_xlpm.multir,_xlpm.e7l), _xlfn.HSTACK("R Square",_xlpm.rsq,_xlpm.e7l), _xlfn.HSTACK("Adjusted R Square",_xlpm.arsq,_xlpm.e7l), _xlfn.HSTACK("Standard Error",_xlpm.sterr,_xlpm.e7l),_xlfn.HSTACK("Observations",_xlpm.count,_xlpm.e7l),_xlpm.el,_xlfn.HSTACK("ANOVA","",_xlpm.e7l),_xlfn.HSTACK("","df","SS","MS","F","Significance F","","",""), _xlfn.HSTACK("Regression",_xlpm.regdf,_xlpm.regss,_xlpm.regms,_xlpm.regf,_xlpm.sigf,"","",""),_xlfn.HSTACK("Residual",_xlpm.resdf,_xlpm.resss,_xlpm.resms,"","","","",""),_xlfn.HSTACK("Total",_xlpm.totaldf,_xlpm.totss,"","","","","",""),_xlpm.el,_xlfn.HSTACK("","Coefficients","Standard Error","t Stat","P-Value","Lower "&_xlpm.conf,"Upper "&_xlpm.conf,"Lower "&_xlpm.conf,"Upper "&_xlpm.conf),_xlfn.HSTACK(_xlfn.VSTACK("Intercept",_xlpm.cl),_xlpm.coeff,_xlpm.serr,_xlpm.tst,_xlpm.p,_xlpm.l95l,_xlpm.u95u,_xlpm.l95l,_xlpm.u95u),_xlpm.el,_xlfn.HSTACK("Observation","Predicted Y","Residuals",_xlfn.DROP(_xlpm.e7l,,1)),_xlfn.HSTACK(_xlpm.obs,_xlpm.pr,_xlpm.res,_xlfn.MAKEARRAY(_xlpm.count,6,_xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,_xlpm.y,"")))),_xlpm.result))

name: BI_RelativeSizeFactor
comment: Returns the Relative Size Factor of given data on any attribute.

name: BI_ReverseText
comment: Returns the text in reverse order.

name: BI_RoundNumbersTest
comment: Returns the Round numbers test in data.
formula: _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.Data,_xlpm.ColumnIndex,_xlpm.Digits,_xlfn.LET(_xlpm.column, _xlfn.CHOOSECOLS(_xlpm.Data,_xlpm.ColumnIndex),_xlpm.den,10^_xlpm.Digits,_xlpm.select,MOD(_xlpm.column,_xlpm.den)=0,_xlpm.filtereddata,_xlfn._xlws.FILTER(_xlpm.Data,_xlpm.select), _xlpm.filteredcolumn, _xlfn._xlws.FILTER(_xlpm.column, _xlpm.select), _xlpm.roundrows, ROWS(_xlpm.filteredcolumn),_xlpm.totalrows, ROWS(_xlpm.Data), _xlpm.roundsum, SUM(_xlpm.filteredcolumn), _xlpm.totalsum, SUM(_xlpm.column),_xlpm.mincolumns, MAX( 2, COLUMNS(_xlpm.Data)), _xlpm.emptyline, _xlfn.MAKEARRAY(1,_xlpm.mincolumns, _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,_xlpm.y,"")),_xlpm.result, _xlfn.VSTACK(IF(COLUMNS(_xlpm.Data)=1, _xlfn.HSTACK(_xlpm.filtereddata,_xlfn.MAKEARRAY(_xlpm.roundrows,1, _xlfn.LAMBDA(_xlpm.x,_xlpm.y,""))),_xlfn._xlws.SORT(_xlpm.filtereddata,_xlpm.ColumnIndex,-1)),_xlpm.emptyline, _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Round Numbers Count",_xlpm.roundrows, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns), _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Total Count",_xlpm.totalrows, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns), _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Round Amount Total",_xlpm.roundsum, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns), _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Total Amount",_xlpm.totalsum, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns),  _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Count %",_xlpm.roundrows/_xlpm.totalrows, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns), _xlfn.TAKE(_xlfn.HSTACK("Amount %",_xlpm.roundsum/_xlpm.totalsum, _xlpm.emptyline),,_xlpm.mincolumns)),_xlpm.result))

name: BI_RowOccurence
comment: Returns how many times the same complete row or selected columns occurs in the data.

name: BI_RunningTotal
comment: Returns Running Total of Data

name: BI_RupeeSymbol
comment: Returns the Rupee Symbol ₹

name: BI_SameSameDifferentTest
comment: Returns Same values in specified columns and different value in third column. Useful in forensic audit.

name: BI_SameSameSameTest
comment: Returns Same values in the given columns (like invoice number, supplier id, employee id). Useful in forensic audit.

name: BI_SLNSchedule
comment: Returns Straight Line Method of Depreciation Schedule.

name: BI_SubsetNumberDuplicationTest
comment: Calculates the Subset Number Duplication Test.

name: BI_Summary
comment: Returns Summary of data according to any attribute.

name: BI_TextSplitByPositions
comment: Returns an array of text split by number of positions as in Power Query.

name: BI_TopN
comment: Returns Top N ranked rows of a column of data.

name: BI_TopPercent
comment: Returns Top N Percentage of Data

name: BI_Unpivot
comment: Returns unpivoted data

name: BI_UnpivotExceptFirst
comment: Unpivot Except First Column

name: BI_WDVSchedule
comment: Returns Written Down Value (Declining Balance) Method of Depreciation Schedule.

name: BI_WordCount
comment: Returns the number of words in text.

name: BI_WorkDay.Indian
comment: Returns the date after a specified number of Indian workdays (Second and Fourth Saturdays as holidays)

name: BI_YearlyCalendar
comment: Returns the Yearly Calendar of any date or current date in horizontal or vertical format.

name: BI_ZScore
comment: Returns the Z Score of data.
